title: Choose Overflow without Hiding Content or Focus
summary: Build and verify css overflow in long translated survey status card incident through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css overflow from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css overflow without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Long translated survey status card incident case 1: overflow hidden is a harmless way to repair any layout that extends beyond a card.

Long translated survey status card incident case 1: identify the overflowing content and sizing cause, preserve access, and use auto, scroll, clip, hidden, or visible only for an intentional contract.
Long translated survey status card incident case 1: apply overflow hidden to the body so no component can cause horizontal scrolling.

steps.title: 04 · Read the long translated survey status card incident case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves css overflow when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: long translated survey status card incident.
steps.why: Choose Overflow without Hiding Content or Focus makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in long translated survey status card incident.

content.text: Overflow visible paints beyond a box, hidden clips and creates a scroll container without visible scrollbars, clip clips without scrolling, auto adds scrolling when needed, and scroll always creates a scrolling mechanism. Clipping can hide text, focus indicators, sticky content, and keyboard destinations. Repair the sizing cause before choosing an intentional overflow behavior. Apply it to long translated survey status card incident.
